task: Write a Python function longest_run_char(s) returning the character of the longest consecutive run of one character. When several runs tie for longest, return the character of the last such run, not the first. An empty string returns ''. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code passes all the given criteria, but an explicit edge case mentioning empty string as input would make it more robust.
trace: First, the function uses Python's `groupby()` function to group consecutive characters. Then, it iterates through each group, counting the number of occurrences and comparing it with the maximum count found so far. When a larger count is found, the corresponding character is updated. If multiple groups tie for the longest run, the last one will be selected because it is the latest in the string. An edge case handled here is when there are no repeated characters in the input string, resulting in an empty result.
